params.name: query
params.description: PromQL expression to evaluate.

params.name: time
params.description: Evaluation timestamp as a Unix timestamp or RFC3339 string. Defaults to current server time.

params.name: timeout
params.description: Evaluation timeout as a duration string (e.g., 30s).

items.docs: Evaluates a PromQL expression at a single point in time. Returns a vector of time series with their values at the specified time. If the time parameter is omitted, the current server time is used.
Evaluates a PromQL expression at a single point in time using a POST request body. Use POST when query parameters exceed URL length limits.

params.name: query
params.description: PromQL expression to evaluate.

params.name: start
params.description: Start of the time range as a Unix timestamp or RFC3339 string.

params.name: end
params.description: End of the time range as a Unix timestamp or RFC3339 string.

params.name: step
params.description: Query resolution step width as a duration string (e.g., 15s, 1m, 1h).

params.name: timeout
params.description: Evaluation timeout as a duration string (e.g., 30s).

items.docs: Evaluates a PromQL expression over a range of time, returning a matrix of time series with values at each step interval. Used for generating graphs and time-based analysis.
Evaluates a PromQL expression over a range of time using a POST request body. Use POST when query parameters exceed URL length limits.

params.name: query
params.description: PromQL expression to evaluate.

params.name: start
params.description: Start of the time range.

params.name: end
params.description: End of the time range.

items.docs: Returns a list of exemplars for a given PromQL expression over a time range. Exemplars are specific trace or log IDs attached to high-value metric observations that link metrics to traces.

params.name: start
params.description: Start of the time range filter.

params.name: end
params.description: End of the time range filter.

params.name: match[]
params.description: Series selector(s) to restrict the label results.

params.name: limit
params.description: Maximum number of items to return.

items.docs: Returns a list of label names present in the TSDB. Optionally filtered by a time range or series selectors. Useful for building autocomplete in query UIs.

params.name: label_name
params.description: The label name to retrieve values for.

params.name: start
params.description: Start of the time range filter.

params.name: end
params.description: End of the time range filter.

params.name: match[]
params.description: Series selector(s) to restrict the label value results.

params.name: limit
params.description: Maximum number of items to return.

items.docs: Returns a list of values for a given label name. Optionally filtered by a time range or series selectors. The special label name __name__ returns all metric names.

params.name: match[]
params.description: Series selector(s). At least one required.

params.name: start
params.description: Start of the time range filter.

params.name: end
params.description: End of the time range filter.

params.name: limit
params.description: Maximum number of items to return.

items.docs: Returns the list of time series that match a certain label set. The response contains the label sets identifying each time series. At least one match[] selector must be provided.

params.name: limit
params.description: Maximum number of items to return.

params.name: metric
params.description: Metric name to filter by. If omitted, all metrics are returned.

params.name: limit_per_metric
params.description: Maximum number of metadata entries to return per metric.

items.docs: Returns metadata about metrics currently scraped from targets. Metadata includes the metric type (counter, gauge, histogram, summary, untyped) and help string. Multiple targets may provide metadata for the same metric.

params.name: match[]
params.description: Series selector(s) to delete. At least one required.

params.name: start
params.description: Start of the time range. Defaults to minimum possible time.

params.name: end
params.description: End of the time range. Defaults to maximum possible time.

items.docs: Deletes data for a selection of series in a time range. This only creates tombstones in the TSDB; actual deletion happens on the next compaction. Requires --web.enable-admin-api flag.

params.name: limit
params.description: Maximum number of items to return.

items.docs: Returns a set of properties about the Prometheus TSDB including the top series by label count, top chunks by label, most frequently changing metrics, and head statistics.

params.name: state
params.description: Filter targets by state.

items.docs: Returns an overview of the current state of the Prometheus target discovery including active and dropped targets, their labels, and scrape health status.

params.name: match_target
params.description: Label selectors matching target labels.

params.name: metric
params.description: Metric name to filter by.

params.name: limit
params.description: Maximum number of items to return.

items.docs: Returns metadata about metrics for individual targets. Allows filtering by target label matchers and metric name to retrieve per-target metadata contributed by scrape pools.

params.name: type
params.description: Filter by rule type.

params.name: rule_name[]
params.description: Filter by rule name.

params.name: rule_group[]
params.description: Filter by rule group name.

params.name: file[]
params.description: Filter by the file the rule group was loaded from.

items.docs: Returns a list of alerting and recording rules currently loaded. Rules include the rule expression, their last evaluation time, and any active alerts for alerting rules.
Returns a list of all active alerts currently firing or pending in Prometheus. Alerts include their labels, state, and timestamps.
Returns an overview of the current state of the Prometheus Alertmanager service discovery including active and dropped Alertmanager instances.
Returns the currently loaded configuration file as a YAML string. Includes the full Prometheus configuration including scrape configs, rule files, and alerting configuration.
Returns the values of all command-line flag settings currently in use by the Prometheus server.
Returns build and version information for the Prometheus server, including version, revision, branch, build user, build date, and Go version.
Returns various runtime properties about the Prometheus server including the start time, storage retention, TSDB stats summary, and corruptible chunk count.
Returns the current status of the WAL (Write-Ahead Log) replay. WAL replay occurs during Prometheus startup to recover data from the most recent checkpoint.
